item.title: SpaceX Rented Out Computing After Own Teams Had Trouble Using It
item.description: SpaceX decided to rent out the full capacity of its Colossus 1 data center in Memphis to Anthropic PBC after encountering technical challenges using the facility to develop and run its Grok artificial intelligence models, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Why ‘Final Fantasy VII Revelation’ Took Only Three Years to Make
item.description: Director Naoki Hamaguchi says the keys were retaining staff and sticking to his vision

item.title: World Cup’s $10 Billion Betting Boom Fuels Market ‘Gamification’
item.description: The World Cup is expected to be the biggest sporting event on the planet. It may also become the industry’s biggest chance yet to turn a global sporting spectacle into an engine for speculative trading.

item.title: Putin Vows to Expand Starlink Rival With Aim to Step Up Attacks
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin said his country is expanding a constellation of domestically produced satellites as he vowed to step up strikes on Ukrainian infrastructure.

item.title: The Four Biggest Issues Facing the Music Industry Right Now
item.description: From AI to fraud to AI-powered fraud, the music business has some concerns

item.title: SpaceX Options to Begin Trading on Tuesday After IPO
item.description: Options contracts on Space Exploration Technologies Corp. will begin trading Tuesday after shares began trading on Friday.

item.title: Rival Space Stocks Tumble as Investors Race Toward Musk’s IPO
item.description: SpaceX’s long-awaited initial public offering triggered a selloff across rocket, satellite and space-linked companies Friday.

item.title: JBS to Close Pennsylvania Beef Plant Amid Cattle Shortage
item.description: JBS NV is closing a beef plant in Pennsylvania, in the latest sign of pressures meatpackers are facing amid a severe US cattle shortage.

item.title: Exxon Studies Takeover Targets Including Woodside Energy
item.description: Exxon Mobil Corp. is studying potential acquisition targets including Australia’s Woodside Energy Group, as the US giant eyes options to deepen its presence in liquefied natural gas and Asian markets, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: Bitcoin Bottom Hunters Are Eyeing Up AI Frenzy and SpaceX IPO
item.description: Bitcoin’s rebound this week from the depths of despair below $60,000 has reignited a familiar Wall Street ritual: trying to call the bottom.

item.title: Middle East Fuel Exports Rebound as Tankers Slip Through Hormuz
item.description: Exports of refined fuels from the Persian Gulf have rebounded this month as more tankers managed to slip through the Strait or Hormuz, providing welcome relief to markets starved of supply.

item.title: Uber Weighs Delivery Hero Asset Sales to Smooth Full Takeover
item.description: Uber Technologies Inc. is reaching out to parties interested in Delivery Hero SE’s regional businesses as it works toward a takeover of the German food delivery company that would get regulatory approval, people with knowledge of the matter said.

item.title: US Consumer Sentiment Picks Up on Easing Gasoline Prices
item.description: US consumer sentiment rose in early June for the first time in four months as lower gasoline prices provided some relief for Americans grappling with a surge in inflation.

item.title: ECB’s Kazimir Says Rates Must Be Lifted More to Tackle Inflation
item.description: Inflation spreading through the economy will force the European Central Bank to raise interest rates further, according to Governing Council member Peter Kazimir.

item.title: SpaceX’s Record IPO Is Said to Draw Over $350 Billion in Demand
item.description: SpaceX’s historic $75 billion IPO drew more than $350 billion in demand from institutions and retail investors, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: HSBC Said to Have $400 Million Exposure to Ailing UAE Firm IFFCO
item.description: IFFCO Group, one of the Middle East’s largest consumer goods companies, has emerged as an unwelcome headache for HSBC Holdings Plc at a time when Chief Executive Officer Georges Elhedery is trying to make the British lender leaner, simpler and more disciplined with capital.

item.title: What Stock Traders Can Expect From SpaceX’s Trading Debut
item.description: Investors will soon have a sense of how SpaceX shares will perform after the largest initial public offering ever — but they may need to wait several hours to get in on the trading action.

item.title: Ken Leech to Plead Guilty in US ‘Cherry Picking’ Fraud Case
item.description: Ken Leech, the former co-chief investment officer at Western Asset Management Co., will plead guilty in a case related to allegations that he fraudulently assigned winning trades to favored clients while forcing others to take losses, days before he was to face a federal criminal trial.

item.title: Chinese Chipmaker MetaX Plans Hong Kong Listing to Seize on Boom
item.description: MetaX Integrated Circuits Shanghai Co. plans to list shares in Hong Kong to capture the boom in chip stocks after a blockbuster initial public offering in mainland China last year.

item.title: Rates Markets ‘Fairly Pricing’ Fed Path, Goldman Trader Says
item.description: A rates-product executive at Goldman Sachs Group Inc. said traders are positioned correctly for the Federal Reserve to raise interest rates to combat a surge in war-driven inflation.

item.title: From London to Sydney, Traders Ready to Watch SpaceX’s Debut
item.description: Initial public offerings don’t normally keep investors awake on the other side of the globe, but SpaceX’s record-breaking IPO is far from ordinary.

item.title: France’s Mistral in Funding Talks at About €20 Billion Valuation
item.description: French startup Mistral AI is in talks to raise around €3 billion ($3.5 billion) at a valuation of roughly €20 billion, according to people familiar with the discussions, providing Europe’s artificial intelligence champion with a cash injection as it competes in a costly computing race against competitors in the US and China.

item.title: Air India Crash Report Delayed as Investigation Continues
item.description: The government agency probing last year’s Air India crash has delayed its final report, saying the investigation into the country’s worst commercial airline disaster isn’t finished yet.

item.title: Morocco Weighs China Free-Trade Agreement, Minister Says
item.description: China asked Morocco to sign a free trade agreement, the North African nation’s industry minister said, as Rabat weighs whether closer economic ties with Beijing would benefit its export-oriented manufacturing sector.

item.title: Taiwan Eyes $200 Million Data Center for Lone South America Ally
item.description: Taiwan plans to help bring a $200 million data center to Paraguay, its lone ally in South America, in a rare investment aimed at shoring up the two partners’ diplomatic relationship that’s backed by Washington.

item.title: IMF Said to Agree on Kyiv Loan Deal Despite Missed Condition
item.description: The International Monetary Fund agreed to release its next tranche of a loan to Ukraine, despite Kyiv’s failure to meet a key condition as the war-weary nation faces a growing backlash against the requirements.

item.title: Gary Gensler Says Kalshi Is Flat Wrong: Sports Bets Aren’t Swaps
item.description: Gary Gensler, overseer of Wall Street for significant parts of the last two decades, has a message for anyone arguing sports betting is a matter for federal financial regulators: They’re wrong.

item.title: Leveraged ETFs Aren’t Just a Korea Problem
item.description: They’re growing in the US too.

item.title: Iran Is Under Mounting Strain From US Naval Blockade
item.description: With ships trapped and oil revenue falling, Tehran has some tough choices ahead.

item.title: Buy Toll, Sell Lennar as Rich Homebuyers Outpace First-Timers
item.description: An economy that’s been good for the rich makes shares of high-end homebuilders a better bet than those focused on entry-level buyers, according to one Wall Street analyst.
